name: Arduino UNO
tagline: The world's most popular open-source microcontroller board
description: The Arduino UNO R3 is the go-to board for learning embedded systems. Based on the ATmega328P, it has 14 digital I/O pins, 6 analog inputs, a USB connection for programming, and runs at 16MHz. It's the foundation for most beginner and intermediate tinkering lab projects.

pinout.name: Analog In
pinout.desc: 10-bit analog to digital input (also usable as digital)

wiringNote: Never draw more than 200mA total from the 5V pin when powered via USB. Use external power via VIN for motor projects.
libraryName: Built into Arduino IDE (no library needed)
libraryInstall: Download Arduino IDE from arduino.cc and select board: Tools → Board → Arduino UNO
commonMistakes: Sourcing > 40mA from a single GPIO pin → burns the pin
Connecting 5V sensor to 3.3V board (Arduino UNO is 5V tolerant — this one is fine)
Forgetting to select correct COM port in IDE
Using delay() with multiple sensors → blocks all other code
